item.title: 呼吁所有 VS Code 用户采取行动！ | Call to action for all VS Code users!
item.description:
今天早些时候，我在VSCodeTips （一个致力于分享如何最好地利用 VS Code 的技巧和窍门的 Forem 社区）上闲逛，并意识到现在是我在 DEV 上放大它的时候了，所以你们其他人可以探索这个相关的和实用社区。 关于您最喜欢的编辑器 VS Code 的社区

除了在 DEV 上跟上 VS Code 的所有内容，我鼓励您前往并加入这个空间，这将使您能够探索子类别，如扩展、主题、快捷方式等。

快乐的 VS 编码！ 最后一件事。 您会注意到“使用 Forem 注册”作为这些 Forem 的一个选项。 这是我们现在提供的一项服务，可以最无缝地导航生态系统并管理您的身份。 我们希望您能够在无需携带全部数据的情况下充分发挥自我，因此从长远来看，使这个分布式生态系统尽可能简单是非常重要的。 创建一个Forem 帐户并通过您的设置将其与您的 DEV 帐户连接。 🙂 🙂



原文:

Earlier today, I was noodling around on VSCodeTips (— a Forem community dedicated to sharing tips & tricks on how to best leverage VS Code) and realized it was high time I amplified it here on DEV so the rest of you can explore this relevant and practical community. VSCodeTips is managed by @nickytonline — a longtime DEV member, contributor to Forem, and former Forem core team member.

A community about your favourite editor, VS Code

In addition to keeping up with all things VS Code on DEV, I encourage you to head over and join this space which will enable you to explore sub-categories like extensions, themes, shortcuts, etc.

👉 Join VSCodeTipes now.

Happy VSCoding! 🎉

One last thing. You’ll notice "Sign up with Forem" as an option on these Forems. This is a service we now offer to most seamlessly navigate the ecosystem and manage your identity. We want you to be able to bring your full self without having to bring your full data, so making this distributed ecosystem as straightforward as possible in the long run is really important. Create a Forem account and connect it with your DEV account via your settings. 🙂
item.author: ben

item.title: 仅 14 行 CSS 中的明暗模式 | Light and dark mode in just 14 lines of CSS
item.description:
我喜欢暗模式所有的东西。 但我也知道很多人喜欢灯光模式！ 为了尊重个人偏好并从一开始就考虑可访问性，我在开始一个新的 Web 项目时添加了对原生明暗模式的支持。

该解决方案不使用 JavaScript ，因此我们没有构建明暗模式切换。 相反，它使用 CSS 媒体查询检测用户的系统设置，并使用两个自定义 CSS 属性来确定基本配色方案。 这是它是如何完成的。

声明 2 个 CSS 自定义属性

您可以在 CSS 文件中的任何位置定义 CSS 自定义属性，它们遵循与其他 CSS 规则相同的级联和特异性模式。 例如，您可以在文档根目录定义 CSS 变量，并在更具体的 CSS 类中覆盖它们。 很棒的是，您还可以在浏览器开发工具中检查和调试声明的 CSS 变量，这些变量显示在样式表规则下方。

您还可以将第二个参数传递给var()函数，如果您尝试使用它时自定义属性不存在，该参数将充当备用值。

 .element { color : var ( --my-new-color , #ff0000 ); }

对于这种明暗模式解决方案，在文档根处定义两个颜色变量——一个用于前景色，一个用于背景色。 我倾向于默认选择深色模式，因此我将背景颜色设置为黑色（ --color-bg ），将前景色设置为白色（ --color-fg ）。

 :root { --color-bg : #000000 ; --color-fg : #ffffff ; }

使用 prefers-color-scheme 媒体查询

接下来，我们将使用prefers-color-scheme CSS 媒体查询挂钩系统设置，并翻转背景和前景色的变量声明。 当检测到浅色主题设置时，以下代码将--color-bg设置为白色，将--color-fg设置为黑色。

 @media ( prefers-color-scheme : light ) { :root { --color-bg : #ffffff ; --color-fg : #000000 ; } }

添加身体样式

最后，使用 CSS 自定义属性，在 HTML body元素上设置background-color （用于页面颜色）和color （用于文本），如果没有被覆盖，所有子元素都将继承它们。

 body { background-color : var ( --color-bg ); color : var ( --color-fg ); }

就是这样——只用 14 行 CSS 就支持原生的明暗模式偏好！

在 CodePen 上查看代码

这是CodePen 的完整示例，它将根据您的系统偏好显示亮或暗模式。 切换您的系统设置以观看 CodePen 切换主题！

你如何实现暗模式和亮模式？ 在推特上告诉我。



原文:

I like to dark mode all the things. But I also know lots of people who prefer light mode! To respect personal preferences and consider accessibility from the start, I add support for native light and dark mode as soon as I begin a new web project.

This solution uses no JavaScript, so we're not building a light and dark mode toggle. Instead, it detects the user's system settings with a CSS media query, and uses two custom CSS properties to determine a basic colour scheme. Here's how it's done.

Declare 2 CSS custom properties

CSS custom properties are also referred to as CSS variables or cascading variables. You can define CSS custom properties anywhere in your CSS files, and they follow the same cascading and specificity patterns as other CSS rules. For example, you can define CSS variables at the document root, and override them in more specific CSS classes. What's great is you can also inspect and debug declared CSS variables in browser dev tools, which appear below the stylesheet rules.

CSS custom properties are declared by words prefixed with two dashes (--), and accessed using the var() function.

:root { --my-color-variable: #000000;
} .element { /* This is calculated as #000000! */ color: var(--my-color-variable);
}

You can also pass a second parameter into the var() function, which acts as a fallback value if the custom property doesn't exist when you try to use it.

.element { color: var(--my-new-color, #ff0000);
}

For this light/dark mode solution, define two colour variables at the document root — one for the foreground colour, and one for the background colour. I tend to choose dark mode by default, so I set the background colour to black (--color-bg) and the foreground colour to white (--color-fg).

:root { --color-bg: #000000; --color-fg: #ffffff;
}

Use the prefers-color-scheme media query

Next, we're going to hook into system settings using the prefers-color-scheme CSS media query and flip the variable declarations for the background and foreground colours. The following code sets the --color-bg to white and the --color-fg to black when a light theme setting is detected.

@media (prefers-color-scheme: light) { :root { --color-bg: #ffffff; --color-fg: #000000; }
}

Add body styles

Finally, using the CSS custom properties, set the background-color (for the page colour) and color (for the text) on the HTML body element, which all child elements will inherit if they're not overwritten.

body { background-color: var(--color-bg); color: var(--color-fg);
}

And that's it — support for native light and dark mode preferences in just 14 lines of CSS!

View the code on CodePen

Here's the full example on CodePen, which will display light or dark mode depending on your system preferences. Toggle your system settings to watch the CodePen switch themes!

How do you implement dark mode and light mode? Let me know on Twitter.
item.author: whitep4nth3r

item.title: Slibro——一个故事写作平台 | Slibro — a story writing platform
item.description:
从这里试用 Android 上的应用程序。

这个想法背后的灵感是简化故事写作、格式化和发布的过程，以覆盖更广泛的受众。 无论您是刚开始写第一个故事还是经验丰富的作家，Slibro 都能满足您的需求！

提交类别：

移动大亨

链接到代码

该应用程序是完全开源的，请查看代码并随时为这个项目做出贡献😉：

一个新的 Flutter 项目。

执照

版权所有 2022 Souvik Biswas

根据 Apache 许可证 2.0 版（“许可证”）获得许可； 除非遵守许可，否则您不得使用此文件。 您可以在以下网址获取许可证的副本

它是如何开始的

我最初是通过创建粗略的 UI 草图和注释来研究这个应用程序的想法......

最近怎么样

该应用程序现在具有成熟的故事编辑器（富文本 - markdown）、故事阅读器和故事管理系统（用于管理已发布/未发布的故事并探索其他出版商的故事）。

以上四个屏幕涵盖了使用电子邮件/密码方法在应用程序上的身份验证流程。 这些屏幕可帮助用户创建新帐户或使用现有帐户登录。 用户注册应用程序后，它会导航到故事创建流程。

这些屏幕帮助用户在 Slibro 应用程序中创建第一个故事作为草稿。 如果需要，用户还可以发布故事。

编辑器和阅读器屏幕是最重要的，编辑器还具有使用 Markdown 的富文本支持。

上面显示的三个屏幕（主页、我的故事和我的个人资料）是应用程序仪表板的一部分，用户可以使用从这些页面访问的导航栏导航到其中的任何一个。

导航栏有一个微妙的动画来改善应用程序的用户体验：

项目介绍

在这个项目中，使用了三个主要的 Appwrite API：

  • 数据库 API ：用于存储与故事和章节相关的应用数据。
  • 使用的 Flutter 插件：

    该项目使用了两个数据库：（1）故事和（2）章节。

    故事数据库文档预览：

    故事数据库属性：

    章节数据库属性：

    文件存储预览（章节存储为 JSON 富文本文件）：

    参考

    • 应用程序文档
    • Appwrite Flutter package Appwrite Flutter 包


原文:

Slibro is a story writing platform that supports short and long format stories. Flutter and Appwrite is used to create this amazing cross-platform experience.

Try out the app on Android from here.

The inspiration behind this idea is to simplify the process of story writing, formatting, and publishing to reach a wider audience. Whether you just getting started with your first story or a seasoned writer, Slibro has got you covered!

Submission Category:

Mobile Moguls

Link to Code

The app is completely open sourced, have a look around the code and also feel free to contribute to this project 😉 :

A new Flutter project.

License

Copyright 2022 Souvik Biswas

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at

http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS, WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied. See the License for the specific language governing permissions and limitations under the License.




How it started

I initially started working on the idea of this app by creating rough UI sketches and notes ...

... also some database structures.

How it's going

The app now has full-fledged story editor (rich text - markdown), story reader, and story management system (to manage published/unpublished stories and explore other publisher's stories).

Some glimpses of Slibro's user interface are as follows:

The above four screens cover the authentication flow on the app using email/password method. These screens help a user to either create a new account or login using an existing account. Once a user signs up to the app, it navigates to the story creation flow.

These screens help a user to create the first story as a draft in the Slibro app. The user can also publish the story if wanted.

The editor and the reader screens are the most important ones, the editor also has rich text support using Markdown.

The three screens (Home, My Stories, and My Profile) shown above are part of the dashboard of the app, user can navigate to any of these using the Navigation bar accessible from these pages.

Navigation bar has a subtle animation to improve the UX of the app:

Project Description

In this project, three of the major Appwrite APIs are used:

  • Account API: Used for implementing user authentication and user data management.
  • Database API: Used for storing app data related to stories and chapters.
  • Storage API: Used for storing the Rich Text story file in JSON format.

The Flutter plugins used:

The Appwrite console view:

Two databases are used for this project: (1) stories, and (2) chapters.

Stories database documents preview:

Stories database attributes:

Chapters database attributes:

The files storage preview (the chapters are stored as JSON rich text files):

  • Appwrite Docs
  • Appwrite Flutter package
item.author: sbis04

item.title: 你能在合并冲突发生之前解决它们吗？ 💥 💥 | Can You Resolve Merge Conflicts Before They Happen? 💥
item.description:
解决合并冲突很有趣！ 没有人喜欢他们，但这并不能改变他们是工作的一部分的事实。 在这篇简短的文章中，我想简要解释它们的来源，展示如何解决它们，并作为额外的奖励，提出一个可能首先避免它们的解决方案！

合并冲突——它们是什么？

合并冲突是当 Git 无法自动解决两次提交之间的代码差异时发生的事件。 它们可能是由于合并分支、rebase 期间或您在 Git 中挑选樱桃时发生的。

好消息是 Git 非常擅长弄清楚如何集成更改，所以大多数时候你是安全的。 当完全相同的行被修改并且 Git 无法确定哪个版本是正确的时，困难的部分就开始了。 它会在终端通知您并让您负责修复它。

以正常方式解决合并冲突

好的，那么我们如何解决冲突呢？ 我们可以手动解决它们，也可以使用 Git GUI，例如内置于 VS Code 或 Jetbrains IDE 中的 GUI，或单独的桌面应用程序，如Tower或Sourcetree 。

让我们看看手动解决冲突。

冲突的更改很容易发现，因为它们将在文件中由<<<<<<<和>>>>>>>标记。 删除不需要的行（包括冲突标记）并保留正确的行。

早期合并冲突检测

合并冲突并不像看起来那么可怕，但事实是解决它们可能是一个漫长而乏味的过程，尤其是在大型项目中。

这就是 GitLive 派上用场的地方 - 一个强大的工具，当您在编辑器中进行与任何其他分支上的更改发生冲突的更改时，它会在您的 IDE 中警告您。 警告向您显示潜在的冲突，并让您有机会在合并冲突发生之前解决它们。

从 GitLive 开始

安装最新的 GitLive VS Code 扩展或JetBrains 插件以开始使用。

当您打开文件时，您会在编辑器的装订线中看到更改指示器，显示您的队友在哪里进行了与您的文件版本相比的更改。 当您和您的队友进行编辑时，这些内容会实时更新。

如果您进行了冲突更改，您将看到亮红色的冲突指示器。 这些冲突可能是您尚未推送的未提交的本地更改，也可能是您的分支上与您的队友的更改发生冲突的现有更改。

在 JetBrains 中，单击作者的头像（或者您可以在 VS Code 中滚动受影响的行）来检查更改并查看差异、它们来自哪个分支，甚至可以直接在本地文件中挑选更改。

就是这样，希望您会发现我的关于如何处理合并冲突的迷你指南很有用，并且下次遇到合并冲突时您不会惊慌。 让我知道您在评论中的想法！



原文:

Solving merge conflicts is fun! - said no one ever. Nobody likes them, but it doesn’t change the fact that they are, well, part of the job. In this short post, I would like to briefly explain where they come from, show how to solve them and as an added bonus, suggest a solution to potentially avoid them in the first place!

Merge conflicts - what are they?

A merge conflict is an event that occurs when Git is unable to automatically resolve differences in code between two commits. They can happen as a result of merging branches, during a rebase or when you’re cherry-picking in Git.

The good news is that Git is quite good at figuring out how to integrate changes, so most of the time you’re safe. The hard part begins when the exact same line has been modified and Git can’t decide which version is correct. It will notify you about it in the terminal and hold you responsible for fixing it.

Solving merge conflicts the normal way

Ok, so how do we resolve conflicts? We can either solve them manually or use a Git GUI such as the ones built into VS Code or Jetbrains IDEs or a separate desktop application like Tower or Sourcetree.

Let’s look at solving conflicts manually.

The conflicting changes are easy to spot as they will be marked in a file by <<<<<<< and >>>>>>>. The part before ======= is your version of the conflicting lines and the part after is their version. Delete the lines you don’t want (including the conflict markers) and keep those that are correct.

Early merge conflict detection

Merging conflicts are not as scary as they seem, but the truth is that solving them can be a long and tedious process, especially on big projects.

That is where GitLive comes in handy - a powerful tool that will warn you inside your IDE the moment you make a change in the editor that conflicts with a change on any other branch. The warnings show you potential conflicts and give you the chance to solve them before the merge conflict occurs.

Starting with GitLive

Install the latest GitLive VS Code extension or JetBrains plugin to get started.

When you open a file you’ll see the change indicators in the gutter of your editor that shows you where your teammates have made changes compared to your version of the file. These update in real-time as you and your teammates are editing.

If you've made a conflicting change you will see the bright red conflict indicator. These conflicts can be uncommitted local changes you have not pushed yet or existing changes on your branch that conflict with your teammates’ changes.

In JetBrains click on the author’s avatar (or you can rollover the affected lines in VS Code) to inspect a change and see the diff, what branch they're from and even cherry-pick changes straight into your local file.

That’s it, hope you’ll find my mini-guide on how to deal with merge conflicts useful and next time you encounter a merge conflict you won’t panic. Let me know what you think in the comments!
item.author: benjamincohen123

item.title: 本周讨论和评论 - v4 | Discussion and Comment of the Week - v4
item.description:
这份每周综述重点介绍了我们认为本周最深思熟虑和/或最有趣的讨论。 我们还将在每一期中突出一个特别酷的评论。 因此，我们希望鼓励人们参与讨论并奖励那些在社区中发起或参与对话的人。 毕竟，社区是通过人们在其中互动而成为可能的。

本周讨论

这周，我感觉我们打成平手了。 所以，共同获奖者@adiatiayu和@joelbonetr ，我希望你们都喜欢分享这个数字奖杯-moji。 这引发了很多人放弃了他们为什么要在公共场合写作和学习的个人看法。 听到对方的声音也很令人耳目一新……很多人都为安静地继续前进提供了坚实的理由。 一个非常普遍的建议是让人们在他们想发言的时候自己决定！

与此同时，Joel 围绕人们最喜欢的可重用功能展开了一场讨论：

看到人们常用的片段列表总是很有趣！

本周评论

那里有很多很棒的评论，但是对于本周的选择，我们将使用@bytebodger的以下评论来回应“ 您如何使用与非开发人员不同的计算机和网络？ ”

这条评论很透彻，读起来很有趣。 作为非开发人员，我发现它也很有帮助。 感谢分享！

你的选择是什么？

这个社区里有很多很棒的讨论和评论。 这些只是我们选择强调的几个。 我敦促大家在下面的评论中分享您最喜欢的评论和/或对过去一周的讨论。 如果你愿意，请给作者一个@mention——它可能会让他们感觉良好。 💚 💚



原文:

This weekly roundup highlights what we believe to be the most thoughtful and/or interesting discussion of the week. We're also be highlighting one particularly cool comment in each installment. 🙌

The DEV Community is particularly special because of the kind and thoughtful discussions happening between community members. As such, we want to encourage folks to participate in discussions and reward those who are initiating or taking part in conversations across the community. After all, a community is made possible by the people interacting inside it.

Discussion of the Week

This week, I'm feeling like we have a tie. So, co-winners @adiatiayu and @joelbonetr, I hope you're both cool with sharing this digital trophy-moji. 🏆 😀

Ayu wrote a really thoughtful prompt asking folks for their opinions on whether or not they believe devs should "put themselves out there":

This triggered lots of folks dropping their personal takes on why they write and learn in public. And it was also refreshing to hear the other side... plenty of folks made a solid case for carrying on quietly. One pretty constant suggestion was to let folks decide for themselves when they wanna speak up!

Meanwhile, Joel fired up a convo around folks' favorite reusable functions:

It's always entertaining to see a list of folks' frequently used snippets!

Comment of the Week

There are so many awesome comments out there, but for this week's selection, we're gonna go with the following comment by @bytebodger in response to "How do you work with computers and the web that is different from non-developers?"

This comment was thorough and just plain ole fun to read. As a non-developer, I found it quite helpful too. 🙌 As others noted in response, the icon-only bookmark bar trick is so dang clever. Thanks for sharing!

What are your picks?

There's loads of great discussions and comments floating about in this community. These are just a few we chose to highlight. 🙂

I urge you all to share your favorite comment and/or discussion of the past week below in the comments. And if you're up for it, give the author an @mention — it'll probably make 'em feel good. 💚
item.author: michaeltharrington

item.title: 未验证、部分和验证的提交 | Unverified, Partially and Verified Commits
item.description:
约克维尤斯特

发表于介绍

世界上最好的VCS （版本控制系统）之一。 作为一个分布式版本控制系统，它使您能够与来自当地社区和全球的其他开发人员共享代码。 使用 GitHub，您可以创建存储库、上传文件和提交更改。

借助所有这些功能，为 GitHub 上的项目做出贡献，并且有时可以在同一个项目中包含这么多人，并且必须正确识别贡献者。 什么是 GPG 签名？

如上图所示，提交类型为Partial 、 Unverified和Verified提交。 让我们看看如何验证提交。

验证提交

生成 GPG 密钥

当提交由 GPG 密钥签名时，会验证提交。 转到您各自操作系统的GPG 命令行工具并安装它。

  • 在您的终端上，键入以下命令以生成 GPG 密钥。
    •  $ gpg --full-generate-key
    1. 在提示符处，指定所需的键类型，或按Enter接受默认值。
    2. 在提示符处，指定所需的密钥大小，或按Enter接受默认值。 您的密钥必须至少为4096位。
    3. 输入密钥应该有效的时间长度。 按Enter指定默认选择，指示密钥不会过期或其他内容，具体取决于您的偏好。
    4. 验证您的选择是否正确。
    5. 输入您的用户 ID 信息，即 GitHub 用户名、电子邮件和令牌。
    6. 键入安全密码。
    7. 使用gpg --list-secret-keys --keyid-format=long命令列出您拥有公钥和私钥的 GPG 密钥的长格式。 签署提交或标签需要私钥。
     $ gpg --list-secret-keys --keyid-format=long
    1. 从 GPG 密钥列表中，复制您想要使用的 GPG 密钥 ID 的长格式。 在本例中，GPG 密钥 ID 为3*4*6*1*5*8... ：
     $ gpg --list-secret-keys --keyid-format=long /Users/hubot/.gnupg/secring.gpg ------------------------------------ sec 4096R/3AA5C34371567BD2 2016-03-10 [expires: 2017-03-10] uid Hubot ssb 4096R/42B317FD4BA89E7A 2016-03-10
    1. 复制您的 GPG 密钥，以-----BEGIN PGP PUBLIC KEY BLOCK-----开头并以-----END PGP PUBLIC KEY BLOCK-----结尾。
    2. 接下来，将 GPG 密钥添加到您的 GitHub 帐户

    将 GPG 密钥添加到您的 GitHub 帐户

    1. 转到您的 GitHub 帐户并单击设置，然后从侧面导航栏转到访问部分。

    2. 单击 SSH 和 GPG 密钥链接后，单击新建 GPG按钮。

    3. 按照生成 GPG 密钥的步骤 10将 GPG 密钥粘贴到文本区域并单击保存按钮。

    1. 要确认操作，请输入您的 GitHub 密码。

    结论

    通过所有这些设置，您可以确保从您的计算机和 GitHub 进行良好的提交并验证提交。 您现在可以提交到您的 GitHub 存储库。 感谢您阅读本教程。



    原文:

    yokwejuste

    Posted on May 13

    Introduction

    GitHub is a pretty awesome platform for sharing code. One of the best VCS(Version control Systems) in the world. Being a distributed version control system, it enables you to share code with other developers from your local communities and across the globe. With GitHub, you can create a repository, upload files, and commit your changes.

    With all these features, contributing to a project on GitHub and can at times include so many people in the same project and proper identification of the contributors is a must. Commits identification on GitHub is is done using a GPG signature.

    What is a GPG signature?

    The GPG(GNU Privacy Guard) signature is a digital signature that is generated using a GPG key. GPG signatures are widely used by Linux package managers such as apt to verify the integrity of downloaded files. GPG signatures are also used by GitHub to verify the integrity of commits. As seen above on the picture above the types of commits Partial, Unverified and Verified commits. Let's see how to verify the commits.

    Verifying the commits

    Generating a GPG key

    A commit is verified when the commit is signed by a GPG key. GitHub Blog explains how to generate a GPG key.

    1. Go to GPG command line tools for your respective operating system and install it.
    2. On your terminal, type the following command to generate a GPG key. 
    $ gpg --full-generate-key
    1. At the prompt, specify the kind of key you want, or press Enter to accept the default.
    2. At the prompt, specify the key size you want, or press Enter to accept the default. Your key must be at least 4096 bits.
    3. Enter the length of time the key should be valid. Press Enter to specify the default selection, indicating that the key doesn't expire or something else depending on your preference.
    4. Verify that your selections are correct.
    5. Enter your user ID information i.e. GitHub username, email and token.
    6. Type a secure passphrase.
    7. Use the gpg --list-secret-keys --keyid-format=long command to list the long form of the GPG keys for which you have both a public and private key. A private key is required for signing commits or tags. 
    $ gpg --list-secret-keys --keyid-format=long
    1. From the list of GPG keys, copy the long form of the GPG key ID you'd like to use. In this example, the GPG key ID is 3*4*6*1*5*8...: 
    $ gpg --list-secret-keys --keyid-format=long
/Users/hubot/.gnupg/secring.gpg
------------------------------------
sec   4096R/3AA5C34371567BD2 2016-03-10 [expires: 2017-03-10]
uid                          Hubot 
ssb   4096R/42B317FD4BA89E7A 2016-03-10
    1. Copy your GPG key, beginning with -----BEGIN PGP PUBLIC KEY BLOCK----- and ending with -----END PGP PUBLIC KEY BLOCK-----.
    2. Next, Add the GPG key to your GitHub account

    Adding a GPG key to your GitHub account

    1. Go to your GitHub account and click on Settings then go to the Access section from the side navbar.

    2. After clicking on the SSH and GPG keys link, click on the New GPG button.

    3. Following the step 10 of the Generating a GPG key Paste the GPG key in the text area and click on the Save button.

    1. To confirm the action, enter your GitHub password.

    Conclusion

    With all this setting you're sure of making good and authenticate commits from both your computer and GitHub. You can now commit to your GitHub repository. Thanks for going through this tutorial.

item.title: 不要犯这些数据科学错误 | Don't do these DATA SCIENCE Mistakes
item.description:
• 花很多时间在理论上。
  • 无需学习先决条件即可直接进入编码 ML 算法。
  • 在不了解基础知识的情况下思考构建未来。
  • 没有花足够的时间来探索和可视化数据。
  • 关注准确性而不是理解模型的工作原理。
  • 假设算法比领域知识更重要。
  • 没有结构化的方法来解决问题。
  • 一次学习多种工具。
  • 没有持续学习/工作。
  • 少交流。
  • 数据泄露。
  • 抽样偏差。
  • 过多的冗余功能。
  • 糟糕的编码风格。
  • 未来功能不可用。
  • 没有进行适当的测试。
  • 没有选择正确的模型 - 验证频率。
  • 选择错误的工具进行可视化。
  • 只关注数据。
  • 在错误的人群上建立模型。
  • 忽略概率。
  • 没有问题/计划的数据分析。
  • 不要卖得好。
  • 不撒谎。
  • 在简历中使用过多的数据科学术语。
  • 高估学位的价值。
  • 不要缩小搜索范围。
  • 比赛不是现实生活。
  • 您的 LinkedIn 个人资料是神圣不可侵犯的。
  • 没有准备好讨论项目。
  • 没有问足够多的问题。
  • 讨论旧项目。
  • 不考虑业务影响。
  • 不擅长技术。
  • 不是解决问题的人。
  • 不从面试官的角度思考。
  • 不以统计数据和事实支持您的陈述。
  • 未能传达您将如何帮助公司。
  • 忘记需求。
  • 不明智地使用“我不知道”。
  • 专注于答案而不是接近。
  • 不抓住机会进入细节。
  • 亲自面对失败。
  • 你认为找工作更容易。
  • 对编码或编程不感兴趣。
  • 你的首要原因是金钱。
  • 你讨厌数学。
  • 总体缺乏激情。
  • 你发现处理数据很烦人。
  • 持续学习很无聊。
  • 缺乏沟通技巧。
  • 讨厌团队协作。
  • 探索和从事较新的项目并不真正吸引你。

Bervianto Leo Pratama - Bervianto Leo Pratama - Apr 15 4月15日



原文:

  • Spending a lot of time on theory.
  • Jumping directly into coding ML algorithms without learning the prerequisites.
  • Thinking to build the future without knowing the basics.
  • Not spending enough time on exploring and visualizing the data.
  • Focusing on accuracy over understanding how the model works.
  • Assuming the algorithm is more important than domain knowledge.
  • Not having a structured approach to problem-solving.
  • Learning multiple tools at once.
  • Not learning/working consistently.
  • Less communication.
  • Data Leakage.
  • Sampling Bias.
  • Too many redundant features.
  • Bad Coding style.
  • Unavailability of features in future.
  • Not performing proper testing.
  • Not Choosing the Right Model- Validation Frequency.
  • Choosing the wrong tool to visualize.
  • Paying Attention Only to Data.
  • Building a Model on the Wrong Population.
  • Ignoring the probabilities.
  • Data Analysis without a Question/Plan.
  • Don't Sell Well.
  • Do Not Lie.
  • Using too many Data science Terms in your Resume.
  • Overestimating the value of academic degrees.
  • Do not narrow your search.
  • Competitions are not Real-Life.
  • Your LinkedIn profile is sacrosanct.
  • Being unprepared to discuss projects.
  • Not asking enough questions.
  • Discussing the old projects.
  • Not considering the business impact.
  • Not good at technical skills.
  • Not being a problem solver.
  • Not thinking from the interviewer perspective.
  • Not supporting your statements with stats and facts.
  • Failing to convey how you will help the company.
  • Forgetting The Requirement.
  • Not Using “I don't know" Judiciously.
  • Focusing on Answer Rather than Approach.
  • Not Taking the Opportunity to go into Details.
  • Taking Failure Personally.
  • You Think Its Easier to get a Job.
  • No Interest in Coding or Programming.
  • Your Primary Reason is Money.
  • You Hate Math.
  • An Overall Lack Of Passion.
  • You Find Working With Data Annoying.
  • Consistent Learning Is Boring.
  • Lack of Communication Skills.
  • Hate Collaboratively Working in a Team.
  • Exploration And Working On Newer Projects does not really appeal to you.

Bervianto Leo Pratama - Apr 15
item.author: codewithsom

item.title: 学习 Rust：一种思维方式 | Learning Rust: A way of Thinking
item.description:
不要失去信心。”

史蒂夫乔布斯。

如你所知，我刚开始接触 Rust，还在学习这门语言，我想知道我应该采用什么样的思维方式。

在我之前关于 RUST 语言的文章发表之后，我注意到一些评论，人们抱怨这种语言的难度。

如果说学习 GO 甚至 JS 就像面临一道屏障，那么 Rust 可能就像一堵墙。

在这篇文章中，我们不会学习如何成为一名更好的开发人员，或者如何使用特定的编程语言进行操作，但我想与您分享我从更有经验的人那里学到的东西。

然而，我真的认为每一次学习都有一个共同的基础：特定的心态和强大的方法论。

冠军心态

心态运动员和心态开发者之间是有区别的。 有心态的运动员是为了赢得它而参与其中的人。 他们有一个单一的想法，专注于他们的目标。 他们受到结果的驱动和激励。

心态开发者是专注于过程的人。 他们对旅程比对目的地更感兴趣。 他们明白过程中存在价值，并且过程是导致成功的原因。

在学习任何新技能时，为任务带来的心态很重要。 在学习新的编程语言时尤其如此。 正确的心态可以决定成败。

在学习一门新的编程语言时，有几件事要记住。
首先，保持耐心很重要。 罗马不是一天建成的，你的编程技能也不会。 学习该语言的新语法和语义需要时间。

第二，做好犯错的准备。 每个人都在制作它们，甚至是经验丰富的程序员。 关键是从你的错误中吸取教训，这样你就不会再犯了。

最后，对自己有信心。 如果您不相信自己可以学习新语言，那么您可能不会。 请记住，您有能力学习任何您下定决心要学习的东西。 以正确的态度，您将立即用您的新语言编写程序。

所以你可以做一些事情来让学习一门新的编程语言更容易，我继续说：

  • 这将帮助您从正确的角度开始并学习该语言的基础知识。 你可以查看我之前的文章，我提到了一些不错的地方。

    • 每个人在学习新事物时都会犯错误。 放松一下，玩得开心。

      • 你使用一门语言的次数越多，你就会越擅长它。 所以找一些也想学语言的朋友一起练习，甚至一起做项目。

        如果您遇到困难，请不要害怕向比您了解更多的人寻求帮助。

        如果你能牢记这些事情，那么你就可以很好地学习一门新的编程语言。 耐心点，犯错，玩得开心，你很快就会成为专业人士。

        制作自己的方法

        有一些通用技巧和技巧可以帮助使学习过程更有效，无论您想学习哪种语言，一般过程都是一样的。

        要做的最重要的事情之一是将任务分解为可管理的块。 试图一次吸收太多信息会让人不知所措，并可能导致很快忘记你学到的东西。 从基础开始，逐步建立更复杂的概念。 找到适合您的学习方法也很重要。

        有些人更喜欢图表和图表等视觉辅助工具，而另一些人则更喜欢通过听录音或阅读文本来学习。

        进行实验，直到找到一种可以帮助您更有效地保留信息的学习方式。

        另一个成功的关键是为自己创造一个积极的学习环境。 确保您处于一个舒适的环境中，并尽量减少干扰。

        设置定期的学习课程也很有帮助，而不是在最后一刻把所有东西都塞进去。 别忘了休息一下！ 试图强迫自己连续几个小时不休息只会导致沮丧和疲劳。

        最后，学习 RUST 时要记住的最重要的事情之一就是对自己要有耐心。 学习是一个过程，只要你付出努力，你最终会到达那里，按照自己的方式去做。

        阿斯米特马拉卡纳瓦尔 - 哈林德·塞拉🇭🇲 - May 2 5月2日



原文:

“Sometimes life is going to hit you in the head with a brick. Don’t lose faith.”

Steve Jobs.

As you know, I just started with Rust and am still learning this language, and I was wondering what the mindset that I should adopt.

Following the publication of my previous article on the RUST language, I noticed some comments in which people complained about the difficulty of this language.

Rust is not an easy language to learn and very different than others that I know. If learning GO or even JS is like facing a barrier, Rust may be like a wall.

In this post We won't learn how to be a better developer, or how to perform in a specific programming language, but I want to share with you what I learned from more experienced people.

However I really think that every learning have a common basis: specific mindset and a strong methodology.

Mindset of Champions

There is a difference between a mindset athlete and mindset developer. A mindset athlete is someone who is in it to win it. They have a one-track mind and are focused on their goals. They are driven and motivated by their results.

A mindset developer is someone who is focused on the process. They are more interested in the journey than the destination. They understand that there is value in the process and that the process is what leads to success.

When learning any new skill, the mindset one brings to the task is important. This is especially true when learning a new programming language. The right mindset can make the difference between success and failure.

There are a few things to keep in mind when learning a new programming language.
First, it is important to be patient. Rome wasn’t built in a day and neither will your programming skills. It will take time to learn the new syntax and semantics of the language.

Second, be prepared to make mistakes. Everyone makes them, even experienced programmers. The key is to learn from your mistakes so that you don’t make them again.

Finally, have confidence in yourself. If you don’t believe that you can learn the new language, you probably won’t. Remember that you are capable of learning anything you set your mind to. With the right attitude, you will be writing programs in your new language in no time.

So There are things you can do to make learning a new programming language easier, I resume them:

  • 1. Find a good tutorial or course.

This will help you get started on the right foot and learn the basics of the language. You can check my previous article, I mentioned some good places.

  • 2. Don't be afraid to make mistakes.

Everyone makes mistakes when they're learning something new. Just relax and have fun with it.

  • 3. Practice, practice, practice.

The more you use a language, the better you'll become at it. So find some friends who also want to learn the language and practice together, or even do project together.

If you're stuck on something, don't be afraid to ask for help from someone who knows more than you do.

If you can keep these things in mind, you will be well on your way to learning a new programming language. Just be patient, make mistakes, and have fun, and you’ll be a pro in no time.

Make your own Method

There are some general tips and techniques that can help make the learning process more effective, no matter which language do you want to learn the general process rest the same.

One of the most important things to do is to break the task down into manageable chunks. Trying to take in too much information at once is overwhelming and can lead to quickly forgetting what you've learned. Start with the basics and gradually build up to more complex concepts. It's also important to find a method of learning that works for you.

Some people prefer visual aids such as diagrams and charts, while others prefer to learn through listening to audio recordings or reading texts.

Experiment until you find a learning style that helps you retain information more effectively.

Another key to success is to create a positive learning environment for yourself. Make sure you're in a comfortable setting with minimal distractions.

It's also helpful to set regular study sessions rather than trying to cram everything in at the last minute. And don't forget to take breaks! Trying to force yourself to focus for hours on end without a break will only lead to frustration and fatigue.

Finally, one of the most important things to remember when learning RUST is to have patience with yourself. Learning is a process, and as long as you're putting in the effort, you will eventually get there, do it your own way.

Asmit Malakannawar - Apr 13

Harinder Seera 🇭🇲 - May 2

item.title: 您从将 CRM 集成到您的产品中学到了什么？ | What have you learned from integrating CRMs into your product?
item.description:
这通常以集成的形式出现，使部分流程自动化 - 跟踪联系信息，记录客户旅程中的重要事件和里程碑，并提醒您在线索变冷之前对其进行跟进。

我最近在 Indiehackers 上遇到了一堆讨论，这些讨论让我想到了 CRM（客户关系管理）软件——当然， CRM 有许多不同的价值主张，每种对创始人和产品支持都有自己的好处团队……但我很好奇您作为开发人员的经历：

您与哪些 CRM 集成？ 是否有任何功能特别重要或非常适合使用？

让我们谈谈 CRM 集成在您的工作中的好处、坏处和丑陋之处。



原文:

CRM stands for “Customer Relationship Management”, and CRM software helps you do just that: it provides tooling to track your interactions and commitments to prospects, customers, and visitors to your app, service, or site. Often this comes in the form of integrations that automate part of the process - keeping track of contact information, logging important events and milestones in the customer journey, and reminding you to follow-up on leads before they go cold.

I’ve come across a bunch of discussions lately on Indiehackers which have had me thinking about CRM (Customer Relationship Management) software - of course, there’s many flavors of CRM with different value propositions, and each has their own benefit to founders and product support teams… but I’m curious about your experience as a developer:

Which CRMs have you integrated with? Do any features stand out as particularly important or great to work with?

Let’s talk about the good, the bad, and the ugly of CRM integration in your work.

item.title: 使用 Ajax jQuery 在 Laravel 中加载更多数据 | Load More Data in Laravel Using Ajax jQuery
item.description:

item.title: Docker 删除所有镜像、卷、容器 | Docker remove all image, volume, container
item.description:
在这里，我将写关于删除 docker 镜像、卷和容器的内容。 为此，首先，您需要关闭 docker 容器。

之后，您可以按照以下方法删除所需的东西，

 # To remove all the containers docker rm -f $( docker ps -a -q ) # To remove all the docker images docker rmi -f $( docker images -a -q ) # To remove all the volumes docker volume rm $( docker volume ls -q ) # To delete everything docker system prune -a --volumes



原文:

Here I am going to write about removing docker images, volumes and containers. To do so, first of all, you need to shutdown the docker container.

After that, you can follow following method to remove required things,

# To remove all the containers
docker rm -f $(docker ps -a -q) # To remove all the docker images
docker rmi -f $(docker images -a -q) # To remove all the volumes
docker volume rm $(docker volume ls -q) # To delete everything
docker system prune -a --volumes

item.title: JavaScript 中的二分搜索算法实现 | Binary search algorithm implementation in JavaScript
item.description:
二进制搜索：- 基于分治法工作的二进制搜索算法。

  • 它用于搜索排序数组中的任何元素。
  • 与线性搜索相比，二分搜索在 O(logn) 的时间复杂度下要快得多，而线性搜索在 O(n) 的时间复杂度下工作。
 function binarySearch(list, item, left, right) { let mid = Math.floor((left + right) / 2) if (left > right) { console.log(left, right) return false } if (list[mid] == item) { return true; } else if (item < list[mid]) { return binarySearch(list, item, left, mid - 1) } else { return binarySearch(list, item, mid + 1, right) } } let list=[1,2,3,5,6,7,8,9]; console.log(binarySearch(list,10,0,list.length-1))

欢迎任何意见和建议。



原文:

Binary Search:- Binary search algorithm which works based on divide and conquer approach.

  • It is used to search for any element in a sorted array.
  • Compared to linear search, binary search is much faster with time complexity of O(logn) whereas linear search works in O(n) time complexity. 
function binarySearch(list, item, left, right) {
    let mid = Math.floor((left + right) / 2)
    if (left > right) {
        console.log(left, right)
        return false
    }
    if (list[mid] == item) {
        return true;
    } else if (item < list[mid]) {
        return binarySearch(list, item, left, mid - 1)
    } else {
        return binarySearch(list, item, mid + 1, right)
    }
}

let list=[1,2,3,5,6,7,8,9];
console.log(binarySearch(list,10,0,list.length-1))

Any comments and suggestions are welcome.
item.author: bvnkumar

item.title: 我本周的错误和教训 | My mistakes & lessons for the Week
item.description:
帕特里克·温多

发表于每个人都会犯错，每个人都会学到新东西。 我想我会记录我的。 可能会把它变成一个每周系列。 所以让我们直接潜入。

得到教训

在工作中，我的任务是创建一个控制器，允许不同类型的用户编辑他们的详细信息。 控制器非常简单 IMO，但路由很棘手。 假设我们有两种类型的用户，帝国和叛军，我们想要的是叛军可以去rebels/info并编辑他们的详细信息，而帝国成员可以去empire/info并编辑他们的详细信息。 通常，可以像这样路由它们

get "/empire/info", to: "/user#edit" get "/rebels/info", to: "/user#edit"

这就是我想做的事情。 但这违反了 DRY 原则，并且基于如何为项目配置路由存在很多范围和命名空间问题，因此我深入研究了文档并发现了路由问题

路由问题允许您声明可以在其他资源和路由中重用的公共路由。

所以现在我添加到 routes.rb 文件中的是这个

concerns :info do get 'info', to '/user#edit' end

一旦我们建立了这种担忧，我们就可以在这样的资源中使用它

resources :empire do concerns :info end resources :rebels do concerns :info end

这解决了我遇到的路由问题。

路由问题！！！ 帮助您保持 DRY了解有关疑虑的更多信息

命名空间控制器和 url 生成问题。

这是一个有趣的。 让我解释。 如果你有一个控制器，比如user_controller.rb并且你想在一个与用户没有任何关联的特定命名空间中使用它的方法之一，那么 rails 搜索控制器的方式可能不是你所期望的。 考虑以下两个代码块 1。 

 resources :avatar do get '/owner/info', to: 'user#show' end

第一个块将在 avatar 目录中搜索 user_controller.rb 文件。 即/avatars/user_controller.rb 。 但是 user_controller 不会在那里。 第二个块看起来很相似，除了在传递文件/user#show之前有一个前导斜杠。 这将告诉 rails 覆盖默认值并遵循该路径。
这篇文章帮助我更好地理解它。

犯的错误

刺激反射、类型转换和传递变量。

我有一个表格，我想通过反射更新数据。 我必须将一些变量传递给反射，以便它可以正确处理正在更新的数据。 我传递了我想要更新的对象的 ID，以及一个布尔值，它将决定一些关于如何更新数据的动态编辑。 问题是那个布尔值。 如果你像这样调用反射

data: {reflex: "click -> "Post#save", is_edited: true } #true here is a boolean

当反射获取数据并检查is_edited变量时，像这样

pp "is_edited = #{element.dataset[:is_edited]}" #results in a String

您的日志将返回true
但问题是，从反射返回的true是字符串而不是布尔值。 因此，如果您尝试将其用作布尔值，它将始终返回 true，因为它不是 nil。

这花了我一段时间才弄清楚，因为通常我们会传递对象的 ID，并且 rails 不会区分Model.find(1)和Model.find("1") （至少在我的经验中没有。）所以类型转换从来都不是我需要考虑的事情。 直到现在。

解决方案很简单，

 is_edited = element.dataset[:is_edited] == true

可能是取回布尔值的更好方法，但是通过这种方式，我完全了解了我要检查的内容。

所以现在我知道要记住刺激反射会输入你发送的数据。

结论

这周我学到了很多。 我最终阅读了从上到下在导轨中路由的文档。 经历了一些自我造成的刺激反射错误，并学会了如何覆盖 Rails 中的一些默认值。 总而言之，这是非常令人兴奋的一周。 干杯。



原文:

Patrick Wendo

Posted on May 13

Everyone makes mistakes, everyone learns something new. I figured I would document mine. Probably make this into a weekly series. So let's dive right in.

Lessons learned

1. Rails Routing Concerns.

At work I was tasked with creating a controller that allowed different types of users to edit their details. The controller was pretty straight forward IMO, but the routing is what was tricky. Say we had 2 types of users, the empire and the rebels, what we wanted is that a rebel could go to rebels/info and edit their details and an empire member could go to empire/info and edit their details. Typically, one could route them like this

get "/empire/info", to: "/user#edit"
get "/rebels/info", to: "/user#edit"

and that's what I thought of doing. But it's against DRY principles and also there were a lot of scope and namespacing issues based off of how the routes were configured for the project so I dived deep into the docs and I found Routing Concerns

Routing concerns allow you to declare common routes that can be reused inside other resources and routes.

So now what I added to the routes.rb file was this

concerns :info do
  get 'info', to '/user#edit'
end

once we have that concern built up, we can use it in a resource like this

resources :empire do
  concerns :info
end

resources :rebels do
  concerns :info
end

and that solves the routing problem I had.

Routing Concerns!!! Helping you to keep DRY Learn more about concerns

Namespaced controller and url generation gotcha.

This was an interesting one. Let me explain. If you have a controller, say user_controller.rb and you want to use one of it's methods in a specific namespace that is in no way associated to the user, the way rails will search for the controller might not be what you expect. Consider the following two blocks of code 1.

resources :avatar do
  get '/owner/info', to: 'user#show'
end

2.

resources :avatar do
  get '/owner/info', to: '/user#show'
end

The first block will search for the user_controller.rb file in the avatar directory. That is, /avatars/user_controller.rb. But The user_controller is not going to be there. The second block looks similar except for the fact that there is a leading forward slash before passing the file /user#show. This will tell rails to override the default and follow that path instead.
This post helped me understand it better.

Mistakes Made

Stimulus Reflex, Typecasting and passing around variables.

I have a form, with which I want to update data through a reflex. I have to pass across some variables to the reflex so it can properly attend to the data being updated. I pass the ID of the object I want to update, and a boolean that will decide some on the fly editing on how the data should be updated. The problem is that boolean. If you call a reflex like this

data: {reflex: "click -> "Post#save", is_edited: true } #true here is a boolean

When the reflex gets the data and you inspect the is_edited variable, like this

pp "is_edited = #{element.dataset[:is_edited]}" #results in a String

Your logs will return true
But the catch is this, the true that is returned from the reflex is a string and not a boolean. As such if you try to use it as a boolean, it will always return true, because it is not nil.

This took me a while to figure out because, typically, we pass across ID's of objects and rails does not differentiate between Model.find(1) and Model.find("1")(At least not in my experience.) So typecasting has never been something I needed to consider. Not until now.

The solution was simple enough,

is_edited = element.dataset[:is_edited] == true

probably a better way to get back your boolean, but this way I understood exactly what I was checking for.

So now I know to remember that stimulus reflex will type cast the data you send across.

Conclusion

I learned a lot this week. I ended up reading the docs for routing in rails top to bottom. Suffered through some self inflicted Stimulus Reflex errors and learned how to override some defaults in Rails. Pretty exciting week all in all. Cheers.
item.author: w3ndo

item.title: 如何在 Ruby C 扩展库中定义异常 | How to define exceptions in Ruby C extension library
item.description:
在 Ruby 语言中，异常是通过继承 StandardError 来定义的。

 class NoIndexError < StandardError ; end

那么如何在 Ruby C 扩展库中定义一个异常类呢？

在 C 中定义一个异常类

在编写 C 扩展时，可以用与 Ruby 语言完全相同的方式定义异常。

 VALUE rb_Bio ; VALUE rb_CGRanges ; VALUE rb_eIndexError ; void Init_cgranges ( void ) { rb_Bio = rb_define_module ( "Bio" ); rb_CGRanges = rb_define_class_under ( rb_Bio , "CGRanges" , rb_cObject ); rb_eIndexError = rb_define_class_under ( rb_CGRanges , "IndexError" , rb_eStandardError ); // ...

调用异常

您可以使用rb_raise 。

 if ( RTEST ( rb_ivar_get ( self , rb_intern ( "@indexed" )))) { rb_raise ( rb_eIndexError , "Cannot add intervals to an indexed CGRanges" ); return Qnil ; }

在这里，实例变量@indexd被检查，如果为真，则引发异常。
写一个测试

使用 test/unit，我写了一个测试如下。 它按预期引发了异常。 

 cgranges . index assert_raises ( Bio :: CGRanges :: IndexError ) do cgranges . add ( "chr1" , 10 , 20 , 0 ) end

就这些。 祝你有美好的一天。

罗德里戈·沃尔特·埃雷斯曼 - Jan 16 1月16日



原文:

In the Ruby language, exceptions are defined by inheriting StandardError.

class NoIndexError < StandardError; end

So how do you define an exception class in Ruby C extension library?

Define a exception class in C

When writing C extensions, exceptions can be defined in exactly the same way as in the Ruby language.

VALUE rb_Bio;
VALUE rb_CGRanges;
VALUE rb_eIndexError; void Init_cgranges(void)
{ rb_Bio = rb_define_module("Bio"); rb_CGRanges = rb_define_class_under(rb_Bio, "CGRanges", rb_cObject); rb_eIndexError = rb_define_class_under(rb_CGRanges, "IndexError", rb_eStandardError);
// ...

Call exception

You can use rb_raise.

 if (RTEST(rb_ivar_get(self, rb_intern("@indexed")))) { rb_raise(rb_eIndexError, "Cannot add intervals to an indexed CGRanges"); return Qnil; }

Here, the instance variable @indexd is checked and if it is true, an exception is raised.
RTEST will determine true or false.

Write a test

Using test/unit, I wrote a test as follows. It raised an exception as expected.

cgranges.index
assert_raises(Bio::CGRanges::IndexError) do cgranges.add("chr1", 10, 20, 0)
end

That is all. Have a good day.

Rodrigo Walter Ehresmann - Jan 16
item.author: kojix2

Using the Quick edit bookmarklet you can make any web site editable. Drag it to your favourites toolbar, click it and you get into edit mode. You can edit, copy and paste, delete and anything else. Hitting ESC will end edit mode.

item.title: 一家科技公司就像一个足球队。 | A tech company it's like a futbol team.
item.description:
拥有、管理或指导一家科技公司，就像一支足球队。 您有一群人为同一目标工作：

可以赢得比赛或赢得每场比赛。

同样，你的科技公司有一群人在尝试或
为同一个目标而工作：盈利、构建/维护产品等。

你是CEO/CTO，你也是导演，你不玩
直接游戏，但你必须看到一切都在轮子上进行。 您与技术总监签约来管理团队，检查一切，您的其他职责是选择最好的球员，您可以支付的费用。 你试图得到最好的天赋、最好的防守或最好的守门员。

同样，科技公司也有这些需求，可以在您的堆栈上寻找最佳工作元素。 足球的优势之一，就是足球的规则，全世界都一样，在技术 Javascript 或 Python 中

在加拿大、墨西哥或萨尔瓦多也是如此。

然后，当你想更上一层楼，更容易获胜时，你可能会聘请一些外国人来获得实力。

您可以超越自己的边界，从外部获得最好的团队成员。 这样做有一些好处：

  • 忠诚度，因为将获得赞助的人会感谢您为他和他的家人所做的一切努力。

  • 多样性，你会带来另一种风格的打法，你的其他玩家会知道一种新的风格。

  • 顶尖人才，你没有限制在你的联赛中工作的一群球员，你可以选择世界上最优秀的人才。

如果您的科技公司想拥有最好的防御或最好的
进攻。

看看https://visto.ai



原文:

Own, manage or direct a tech company it's like a futbol team. You have a group of person working for the same objective:

Could be won a tournament or win each game.

In the same way, your tech company has a group of people trying or
working for the same objective: make a profit, build/maintain a product, etc.

You are the CEO/CTO and you are the director too, you don't play
directly the games, but you have to see everything goes on wheels. You contract a Technical Director to manage the team, to check everything and other of your responsibility is select the best players what you can pay. You try to get the best talent, the best defence or the best goalkeeper.

In the same way, the tech companies have these needs, to search for the best elements for work on your stack. And one of the advantages of the futbol, it's the rules of the futbol, it's the same around the world, in tech Javascript or Python

is the same in Canada, Mexico or Salvador.

Then, when you want to go to the next level and win easier, you might hire some foreigners to get strength.

You can look out of your border limits to get the best team player you can get from outside. Doing that you have some advantages:

  • Loyalty, because the person who will receive sponsorship, will be thankful for all the effort you did to bring to him and his family.

  • Diversity, you will bring the style of playing from another side, your others players will know a new style.

  • Top Talent, you have not restricted a group of players working in your league, you can select the best talent in the world.

If your tech company wants to have the best defense or the best
offensive.

Take a look at https://visto.ai
